steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Words in context. The passage describes a painter who is passive and naïve and increasingly compromises his art at the urging of exhibitors, praisers, and purchasers. These outside parties now "determine" his work — meaning they take control of what it becomes.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: The escalation from demanding proliferation to determining the work points to imposed control, not judgment or choice. The word that means "to control or impose authoritatively" is "dictate."

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — Conclude: means to bring to an end or infer; it does not capture the idea of outsiders controlling the content of the art. ✗
  • Choice B — Dictate: means to control or prescribe authoritatively, matching how exhibitors and buyers now shape his work. ✓
  • Choice C — Evaluate: means to assess or judge, which is what critics do; the passage describes control, not judgment. ✗
  • Choice D — Select: means to pick from options; the buyers do not merely choose among finished works, they shape what the work becomes. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
